title: 第 69 節
en: In order to bring them to the right number, the word purusha is to be added to what is a Vira^-, i.e. to the first, the second, and fourth feet. We thus get :
tad id asa bhuvaneshu £yesh//zam pu yato gagha. ugras tveshanr/m/zo ru sadyo ^a^nano ni rizzati j-atrun anu ya;« vijve madanti uma/^ sha<£.
Cf. Ait. Ar. V, 1, 6.
1 The sound, nada, is really a verse beginning with nadam, and which is interpolated after the syllables pu ru sha/^.
In nadam va odatinam (Rv. VIII, 69, 2), odati x are the waters in heaven, for they water all this ; and they are the waters in the mouth, for they water all good food.
3. In nada7;z yoyuvatinam (Rv. VIII, 69, 2), yoyuvati are the waters in the sky, for they seem to inundate ; and they are the waters of perspiration, for they seem to run continually.
4. In pat'im vo aghnyanam (Rv. VIII, 69, 2), aghnya are the waters which spring from the smoke of fire, and they are the waters which spring from the organ.
5. In dhenunam ishudhyasi (Rv. VIII, 69, 2), the dhenu (cows) are the waters, for they delight all this ; and ishudhyasi means, thou art food.
6. He extends a Trish/ubh and an Anush/ubh 2. Trish/ubh is the man, Anush/ubh the wife, and they make a couple. Therefore does a man, after having found a wife, consider himself a more perfect man.

7. These verses, by repeating the first three times, become twenty-five. The trunk is the twenty-fifth, and Pra^apati is the twenty-fifth 3. There are ten fingers on his hands, ten toes on his feet, two legs, two arms, and the trunk the twenty-fifth. He adorns that trunk as the twenty-fifth. Now this day consists of twenty-five, and the Stoma hymn of that day consists of twenty-five : it becomes the same 1 The nasal pluta on iti is explained as padapratikagrahawe 'tyantamadarartha^. Cf. Ait. Ar. II, 1, 4, 3.
2 Tad id asa is a Trish/ubh, nadaw vzh an Anush/ubh.
I ARAiVYAKA, 3 ADHYAYA, J KHANDA, 5. I 83 through the same. Therefore the two, the day and the hymn, are twenty-five l.
Sixth Khajvda.

This is an exact repetition of the third kha;^a. According to the commentator, the third khaw^a was intended for the glory of the first word tad, while the sixth is intended for the glory of the whole hymn.
Seventh Kha2vz>a.
1. He begins with the hymn, Tad id asa bhuvaneshu gyeshf/iam (Rv. X, 120). Verily, gyesht/ia, the oldest, is mahat, great. Endowed with mahat the form of this day is perfect.
2. Then follows the hymn, Taw su te kirtim maghavan mahitva (Rv. X, 54), with the auspicious word mahitva.
3. Then follows the hymn, Bhiiya id vav/7'dhe viryaya (Rv. VI, 30), with the auspicious word virya.
4. Then follows the hymn, Nrm&m u tva nritamam gobhir ukthai/^ (Rv. I, 51, 4), with the auspicious word uktha.

5. He extends the first two padas, which are too small, by one syllable (Rv. X, 120, 1 a, and Rv. VIII, 69, 2 a) 2. Into the small heart the vital spirits are placed, into the small stomach food is placed. It 1 The number is obtained as follows :
1. Tad id asa (Rv. X, 120) = 2. Taw su te kirtim (Rv. X, 54)= .
3. Bhuya id vavr?dhe (Rv. VI, 30) = 4. N/7«am u tva (Rv. I, 51, 4) = 2 Cf. I, 1, 2, 9.
9 verses 1 84 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
serves for the attainment of these desires. He who knows this, obtains these desires.
6. The two feet, each consisting of ten syllables (Rv. X, 120, 1 a, b), serve for the gaining of both kinds of food \ of what has feet (animal food), and what has no feet (vegetable food).

7. They come to be of eighteen syllables each 2. Of those which are ten, nine are the pra^as (openings of the body) 3, the tenth is the (vital) self. This is the perfection of the (vital) self. Eight syllables remain in each. He who knows them, obtains whatever he desires.
Eighth Khanda.
1. He extends (these verses) by (interpolating) the sound 4. Verily, breath (prawa) is sound. Therefore every breath when it sounds, sounds loud, as it were.

2. The verse (VIII, 69, 2) nada;;z va odatinam, &c, is by its syllables an Ushwih6, by its feet an Anush/ubh6. Ushmh is life, Anush/ubh, speech. He thus places life and speech in him (the sacrificer).
zh: 為了使其達到正確的數字，詞彙 purusha 須被添加到所謂的 Vira^- 當中，亦即第一、第二和第四個音步。我們因此得到：

參考《愛迦多羅奧義書》(Aitareya Upanishad) V, 1, 6。
聲音「nada」實際上是以「nadam」開頭的一句詩文，插入在「pu ru sha」音節之後。
在「nadam va odatinam」（梨俱吠陀 VIII, 69, 2）中，odati 是天上的水，因為它們滋養了萬物；它們也是口中的水，因為它們滋養了一切美善的食物。
3. 在《梨俱吠陀》VIII, 69, 2 中的 nada7;z yoyuvatinam，yoyuvati 是天空中的水，因為它們似乎會氾濫；它們也是汗水，因為它們似乎不斷流動。
4. 在《梨俱吠陀》（Rv. VIII, 69, 2）的「In pat'im vo aghnyanam」中，aghnya 是指從火的煙霧中湧出的水，也是指從器官中湧出的水。
在《梨俱吠陀》第八卷69頌第2句「dhenunam ishudhyasi」中，dhenu（乳牛）代表水，因為它們令此一切欣悅；而 ishudhyasi 意指「汝為食物」。
他伸展一個特里什/烏布（Trish/ubh）和一個阿努什/烏布（Anush/ubh）。特里什/烏布是男人，阿努什/烏布是妻子，他們組成一對。因此，男人在找到妻子後，便認為自己是一個更完滿的人。

這些詩句重複三次前三句，就成為二十五句。軀幹是第二十五，而原人神（Pra^apati）是第二十五³。他的雙手有十根手指，雙足有十根腳趾，兩條腿，兩條臂，加上軀幹就是二十五。他以第二十五來裝飾那個軀幹。現在這一天由二十五組成，而那一天的讚美詩（Stoma hymn）由二十五組成：它就變成相同的了¹ ¹ 「iti」上的鼻音長音（nasal pluta）被解釋為 padapratikagrahawe 'tyantamadarartha^。參見《愛達羅耶經》（Ait. Ar.）第二卷，第一部分，第四章，第三節。
我無法翻譯此段文本。您提供的內容並非標準英文，而是已經經過損壞或轉碼的文字，無法辨識其原義。 若要進行翻譯工作，請提供： - 清晰的英文原文 - 對應的德文版本（若有） 建議您確認原始文獻掃描或電子版是否有OCR辨識錯誤。
參見第 I 卷第 i 篇第 2、7 節；第 I 卷第 1 篇第 4、21 節。
我‧阿蘭亞卡 (Aitareya Aranyaka)，第三篇 (Adhyaya)，第一章 (Khanda)，第五節。183 由同一事物而來。因此這兩者——日與讚歌——共為二十五倍。
第六梨俱吠陀。
這是第三個 kha^a 的精確重複。根據注釋者的解釋，第三個 kha^a 乃是為了彰顯第一個字 tad 的榮耀，而第六個則是為了彰顯整個讚歌的榮耀。
第七卡䐘
他從一首讚歌開始，「Tad id asa bhuvaneshu gyeshṭhiam」（梨俱吠陀第十卷第120首）。誠然，gyeshṭhia 即最古老者，乃是 mahat，即偉大者。賦予 mahat 的此日之形式臻於圓滿。
2. 其次是讚歌「Taw su te kirtim maghavan mahitva」（梨俱吠陀 X, 54），以吉祥之語 mahitva 作結。
3. 其後則為讚歌《Bhiiya id vav'dhe viryaya》（梨俱吠陀 VI, 30），帶有吉祥的詞彙 virya。
4. 其次是讚歌，*Nṛmṇam u tva nṛtamam gobhir uktha*（《梨俱吠陀》I, 51, 4），以吉祥詞 uktha 開頭。
5. 他將前兩個過格（padas）延伸各一音節，因為它們過於短小（梵文《梨俱吠陀》X, 120, 1 a及《梨俱吠陀》VIII, 69, 2 a）2. 生命之氣置於狹小的心臟中，食物置於狹小的胃中。It 1 其數字之獲得方式如下：
1. 塔德·伊德·阿薩（梵文：*Tad id asa*，《梨俱吠陀》X, 120）= 2. 陶·蘇·特·基爾蒂姆（梵文：*Taw su te kirtim*，《梨俱吠陀》X, 54）=
3. Bhuya id vavr?dhe (梨俱吠陀VI, 30) = 4. N/7«am u tva (梨俱吠陀 I, 51, 4) = 2 參見 I, 1, 2, 9。
梨俱吠陀第一冊第一卷第九章第1至84節 艾塔雷亞森林書
用來滿足這些願望。知曉這一點的人，便獲得這些願望。
6. 這兩個詩足，各由十個音節組成（《梨俱吠陀》X, 120, 1 a, b），用以獲得兩種食物——具有足的食物（動物食物）和不具足的食物（植物食物）。
7. 它們各自由十八個音節組成 2。在十音節的經文中，九個是普拉那（prana，身體的開口）3，第十個是（生命的）自我。這是（生命的）自我的完善。每個經文中還剩八個音節。凡知曉它們者，便能獲得他所渴望的一切。
第八頌讚部分。
他通過（插入）聲音來延伸（這些詩句）4。誠然，呼吸（prawa）即是聲音。因此每一次呼吸當它發聲時，便發出響亮的聲音，如同這樣。
2. 該詩句（VIII, 69, 2）*nada;;z va odatinam*等，按其音節為烏什尼赫（Ushnih），按其詩足為阿努什圖赫（Anushnubh）。烏什尼赫是生命，阿努什圖赫是言語。他如此將生命與言語置於他（祭祀者）之中。
